description: Attack hitbox system is completely broken. It does not render in the editor, is not saved on close, and does not exist at runtime after being added. Assume both hitbox and attack box systems are affected. Codex must analyze the attached screenshot to understand the issue, then trace the full pipeline including creation, rendering, serialization, and runtime loading. Identify and fix the root cause so hitboxes function correctly in both editor and runtime.

type: make sure child assets are rendered so that the bottom center of the child asset is placed at the anchor point position. currently the child assets are rendered with their bottom left corner at the anchor point position which is incorrect and causes a lot of issues with placement and rotation of child assets. make sure the child assets are rendered with their bottom center at the anchor point position so that they are properly aligned and rotated around the anchor point.

frame editor onion shit for the frame editor modes. give each frame editor mode an option to turn on onion layers so we can see the previus and next position of points (slightly opaque and non editable) we dont need this for movment since we see all the points just hitboxes and anchor points
when we enter the asset info stack disable movment debuigger
last_updated: anchor point debugger initially if they were on. when cycled to anchor point editor turn it back on (anchor point debugging) auto matically
severity: when we exit anchor point editor turn it off. movment debugger stays off while in the frame edditor stack. on exit of the asset info / frame editor stack return to whatever settings the user had.

type: for the anchor point editor in the left pannel have a text box that displays the current depth offset of a point update it as its chnaged. lets the user type in it to change that value.

anchor point rotation value we can enter adjust in the anchor point editor any asset that is bound to that point should be rotated at that value. it will be a text box value we can adjust. visually we dont need to display any rotation as its the asset binded to it that will get rpotated

rotattion of hit box and attack box in editor we can rotate the full rectanle to keep it rectangular cleanly. maybe like if we have the box selected there will be like a little node we can click to rotate it
anchor point is_inverted value (so we know that this anchor point was inverted and if an asset is bound to it we can invert the asset apropriatly and invert the rotation.)
